title: PM-Schema-004 Handoff Demotion
objective: Normalize the parent-root `pm-schema-004` draft-publication handoff so it no longer presents itself as a still-live queue step after standalone repo cutover and instead acts as historical packet provenance with explicit current-routing redirects.
dependency_note: After Packet 111 demoted the parent-root `pm-schema-003` draft-publication handoff, the adjacent `pm-schema-004` handoff remained the next strongest current-looking residue in the same family. Its scope line was already historical, but the title, summary wording, `Why This Packet Is Next`, and follow-on section still read like a live queue step.
execution_scope: Retitle the pm-schema-004 draft-publication handoff as historical provenance.
Add an explicit historical note and current-routing block.
Relabel the live queue headings and present-tense queue wording without rewriting the full historical body.
forbidden_scope: broad handoff-archive rewriting beyond top-of-file demotion and heading normalization
success_criteria: The pm-schema-004 draft-publication handoff now reads as historical provenance instead of a live queue step.
The file explicitly routes current readers to `PROJECT_STATUS.md` and the closeout inventory.
Repo-owned status routing records the closure and keeps the next lane on remaining individual draft-publication handoffs and packet-history index surfaces.

Historical Why This Packet Was Next$','^## 9\. Historical Follow-On After This Packet$'

Select-String -Path ops/agents/handoffs/2026-04-22-parent-root-pm-schema-004-draft-publication-handoff.md -Pattern '^# Parent-Root PM-Schema-004 Draft Publication Handoff$','^## 2\. Why This Packet Is Next$','^## 9\. Follow-On After This Packet$'
next_action: Return repo-foundation routing to the remaining individual draft-publication handoffs and packet-history index surfaces that still advertise active or current queue wording at the top of the file.
